landingPage.name: Youssef Abouyahia
landingPage.resumeText:
Cybersecurity Engineer passionate about pentesting, I nurture this passion by regularly playing CTFs.

Creator of automations, I love building n8n automations to simplify my daily life. Whether through connected IoT devices or custom n8n workflows, I constantly seek to automate and optimize my tasks. My next challenge: learning to hack these IoT devices to better understand their security. This is why I am looking for an internship to fund this passion.

forensique.title: Forensics
forensique.contentText: Advanced skills in digital forensics: data acquisition, evidence analysis, and development of analysis tools.

reverse.title: Reverse Engineering
reverse.contentText: Strong skills in static/dynamic binary analysis and malware deobfuscation.

devsecops.title: DevSecOps
devsecops.contentText: Deployment and hosting of tools and solutions while taking into account IT security and regulatory compliance. Proficient in CI/CD and network security.

domotique.title: Home Automation
domotique.contentText: Local hosting of home automation solutions. Installation, creation, and connection of home automation devices to automate repetitive daily tasks.

developpement.title: Development
developpement.contentText: Expertise in Java, Python, C/C++, Javascript, Bash, Docker, and more. Advanced use of Github for version management and collaboration on personal and professional projects.

aboutMePage.title: WHOAMI?
aboutMePage.paragraph_one: Master 2 student in Cybersecurity and Crisis Leadership at ESILV, I am currently looking for an internship for April/May 2026.
aboutMePage.paragraph_two: State Engineer graduate in cybersecurity and passionate about pentesting, I nurture this passion by regularly participating in CTFs.
aboutMePage.paragraph_three: Passionate about IoT, I have completed several connected device projects and am heavily invested in automating daily tasks. In my free time, I explore hardware hacking and learn how to break these small IoT devices to better understand their security. However, IoT can be costly, and to pursue this passion of analyzing and exploiting the vulnerabilities of these devices, I need to fund the purchase of equipment. This is one of the reasons why I am actively seeking an internship.
